title: SyntheticKnowledgeCorpus
description: Versioned retrieval corpus for Kira AI knowledge cards. Each entry is an atomic document suitable for indexing in Azure AI Search (vector + lexical hybrid mode).

generatedAtUtc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of corpus generation.

entries.description: Ordered list of retrievable knowledge entries.

id.description: Stable, globally unique card ID in kebab-case. Used for deduplication and as the Azure AI Search document key.

title.description: Short human-readable card title shown in the rep UI.

content.description: Full policy or procedure text used for vector embedding and AI grounding. Not displayed directly in the UI.

snippet.description: One to two sentence summary displayed on the knowledge card in the rep UI.

sourceUrl.description: Canonical URL to the full policy or SOP document.

recommendedAction.description: Concrete action the rep should take, shown in the Next Best Action panel.

category.description: Card category used for UI grouping and Azure AI Search filter facets.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale this entry was authored for, e.g. 'en-US'.

version.description: Entry version, e.g. '1.0'. Increment on any content change.

lastUpdatedUtc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the last content update.

riskLevel.description: Operational risk if this card scenario is mishandled or ignored.

escalationRequired.description: When true, the rep must escalate to a supervisor or a dedicated safety team before attempting resolution.

intents.description: Customer intent labels expected to trigger this card (e.g. 'complaint.delivery', 'intent.cancel'). Used by the AI retriever for intent-based routing.

entities.description: Named-entity types expected in utterances that should trigger this card (e.g. 'competitor', 'product.propane'). Used by the AI retriever for entity-based scoring.

keywords.description: Lexical keywords used by the lightweight SyntheticCorpusLoader for keyword-match scoring in mock mode, and as lexical boost terms in Azure AI Search hybrid queries.
